response.justification: This candidate fails to meet two critical mandatory requirements: only 6 months of professional C++ experience (vs. 5–10 years required) and zero exposure to capital markets, investment banking, or fixed-income domains. While they demonstrate strong software craftsmanship, senior-level ability, and excellent communication skills, these strengths cannot compensate for the absence of required technical and domain expertise in a consulting engagement with tight domain constraints.
strengths: Staff-level seniority with proven ability to architect systems and mentor others across multiple disciplines
Strong track record of communication, teaching, and knowledge-sharing through workshops and community engagement
Demonstrated mastery of software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD pipelines, code quality, and trunk-based development
concerns: Only 6 months of professional C++ experience at National Instruments; mandatory requirement is 5–10 years of hands-on development
No Windows platform development experience; career entirely focused on web, mobile, open-source, and embedded systems on Linux/macOS
Zero exposure to capital markets, investment banking, fixed-income trading, or derivatives; no pathway to acquire this domain knowledge quickly in a consulting role
